question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The unemployment rate gap between Delaware and Michigan might exceed that between Idaho and Iowa.
options.B: The highlighted states have highly diverse unemployment rate levels overall.
options.C: Delaware and Michigan have similar unemployment rates.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Delaware (9.2%) and Michigan (11.8%) have a 2.6% unemployment rate difference, while Idaho (5.6%) and Iowa (6.3%) have only a 0.7% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Delaware and Michigan into the same color bin (8%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Idaho and Iowa appear more different due to different color bins.
